title: adenostyles glabra var. australis
intro: Adenostyles glabra var. australis, also known as Hoary Cudweed, is a perennial plant native to Australia, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and hairy stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Hoary Cudweed include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Hoary Cudweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
